title: ABC谋杀案
summary:
Hastings and Hercule Poirot discuss the tropes of detective fiction, with Hastings suggesting sensational elements like mysterious daggers or beautiful women, while Poirot argues that the most profound crimes are often private and simple. Their conversation is interrupted by a phone call from Japp, who reports a murder in Andover. Poirot informs Hastings that an elderly tobacconist named Mrs. Asher has been killed. Although Hastings initially finds the death of a small shopkeeper unexciting, he agrees to accompany Poirot to Andover to examine an anonymous letter Poirot received.

In Andover, Poirot and Hastings meet Inspector Glen. A constable named Dover discovered Mrs. Asher's body at 1:00 a.m. in her shop; she had been killed by a severe blow to the back of the head. Based on customer sightings, the time of death is estimated to be between 5:30 and 6:05 a.m. The primary suspect is her estranged husband, Achille Asher, a German alcoholic who had previously threatened her. Inspector Glen notes that nothing was stolen from the shop. 

Upon reviewing the anonymous letter, Inspector Glen observes that the handwriting is too clear to have been written by Achille, whose body is paralyzed and prone to shaking. The inspector also notes the high quality of the paper and ink used. Poirot expresses concern regarding a coincidence in the letter, which mentions the date of the twenty-first.
